title: Make Greenland Great Again Act
plain_summary: This bill lets the President try to buy Greenland from Denmark starting on January 20, 2025. If the President makes a deal with Denmark, he must send it to Congress within five days. Congress then has 60 days to look at the deal and decide if they want to stop it. If Congress does not stop the deal within 60 days, it will happen automatically.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Make Greenland Great Again Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Authorization to Enter Into Negotiations with the Kingdom of Denmark
sections.text: Congress gives the President permission to start talks with the Kingdom of Denmark to buy Greenland for the United States. The President can begin these talks starting at twelve zero one in the afternoon Eastern Standard Time on January twentieth, two thousand twenty five. If the President makes a deal with Denmark to buy Greenland, the President must send the agreement to the right committees in Congress within five days after the deal is made. The agreement must include all related papers and attachments. Congress will have sixty days to review any agreement to buy Greenland from Denmark. This sixty day period starts when the President sends the agreement to the congressional committees. If Congress does not pass a joint resolution of disapproval within the sixty days, then the agreement becomes law and takes full effect. The appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Foreign Affairs in the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations in the Senate.
